substep.title: Organize Sub-Questions into Waves
substep.action: Group independent sub-questions into the same wave (parallel dispatch). Place a sub-question that depends on a prior finding in a later wave. The common dependency is the two-stage thoughts pattern: thoughts-locator (wave 1) surfaces candidate artifacts; thoughts-analyzer (wave 2) reads the ones worth reading. Most investigations are one or two waves.

check.if: any sub-question routes to web-search-researcher AND INTERACTIVE
check.ask: Name the sub-question(s) needing web research. Ask the user to approve dispatching web-search-researcher, recommended option first. If declined, drop those sub-questions and record them as deferred gaps for the Open Questions section.

check.if: any sub-question routes to web-search-researcher AND AUTONOMOUS
check.action:
If web_search_approved is true, keep the web sub-questions. If false or absent, emit to stdout and stop:
CLARIFICATION_NEEDED: Investigation requires web research for {sub-question(s)}, but web_search_approved was not set. Re-dispatch with web_search_approved=true, or narrow the question to internal sources.

substep.title: Dispatch Each Wave
substep.action: For each wave in order: dispatch all of the wave's child prompts via the Task tool in a SINGLE message (parallel within the wave). Compose each prompt per child-subagent-dispatch prompt-anatomy — the sub-question alone, never the parent task's intent. Wait for the whole wave to return before starting the next wave, so a later wave can use earlier findings (e.g., thoughts-analyzer prompts name the artifacts thoughts-locator surfaced).

substep.title: Validate Returns and Sharpen-Redispatch
substep.action: Inspect each child return. Redispatch any vague return ("found some files", "seems like") with a sharper prompt before moving on. A "no matches" return is a negative finding, not a vague one — keep it; it becomes a cited finding in step 4. Collect the raw findings per sub-question for synthesis.
